---
title: "Connor Wolosewicz"
page-layout: full
image: pfp3.jpg
image-shape: rounded
image-width: 22em
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/connor-wolosewicz
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:connorwolosewicz@gmail.com
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/connor-wolosewicz
---

## About Me

I’m Connor, and I'm an aspiring urban planner in the Chicago area hoping to make cities more sustainable. I recently graduated summa cum laude from George Washington University with a BS in Environmental and Sustainability Science and a second major in Public Policy. 

There, I gained a comprehensive understanding of socio-ecological systems and the policy levers available to govern them. I’ve previously applied this in my research on DC’s regulations on green infrastructure, including the progressive Green Area Ratio, and the interaction between roadside stormwater gardens and rodent infestation. While studying abroad in Sydney, I used GIS and Microsoft Suite to improve the legibility of planning codes with an AI tool. Ultimately, I aim to bring these skills and cosmopolitan understanding of urban ecology to work as an environmental municipal planner or consultant.

Feel free to explore this page. I adapted it from a blog I made for an introduction to data science class. For any inquiries, feel free to reach out via email or LinkedIn using the links to the left.
